title: Configuration Change Audit Trail

compliance.assurance_rationale: Records configuration, provisioning, and template-related changes made through Catalyst Center, supporting change control evidence.
compliance.controlObjective: Helps ensure configuration changes to managed infrastructure are visible and attributable, aligning with NIST 800-53 CM-3 (configuration change control) for the operations plane.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search `UC-5.13.46` with export or dashboard snapshot tied to a change record when reviewing CM-3 effectiveness.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Exposes who initiated configuration and provisioning work on in-scope network equipment for reconciliation with approved change records.
compliance.controlObjective: Supports matching Catalyst Center-originated changes to authorized change management tickets for financial-reporting–relevant network controls.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Monthly CSV or scheduled PDF from `UC-5.13.46` cross-walked to the change-ticket list in the SOX control sample.

dataSources: index=catalyst, sourcetype cisco:dnac:audit:logs (Catalyst Center audit; configuration, provisioning, template-related auditRequestType values)
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:audit:logs" (auditRequestType="CONFIG*" OR auditRequestType="*PROVISION*" OR auditRequestType="*TEMPLATE*") | stats count as change_count values(auditDescription) as actions by auditUserName, auditIpAddress | sort -change_count
description: Tracks all configuration changes made through Catalyst Center, including template deployments, provisioning actions, and policy changes.
value: Configuration changes are the leading cause of network outages. A complete audit trail enables rapid rollback and root-cause identification.
implementation: Enable the `audit_logs` input in the Cisco Catalyst TA pointing to `index=catalyst`. The TA polls audit log data from the Catalyst Center Intent API `/dna/intent/api/v1/audit/logs` every 5 minutes. Key fields: `auditRequestType`, `auditDescription`, `auditUserName`, `auditTimestamp`, `auditIpAddress`.
visualization: Table (auditUserName, auditIpAddress, change_count, actions), timechart of config-related events, drilldown to raw events for ticket correlation.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7538).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: index=catalyst, sourcetype cisco:dnac:audit:logs (Catalyst Center audit; configuration, provisioning, template-related auditRequestType values).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable the `audit_logs` input in the Cisco Catalyst TA pointing to `index=catalyst`. The TA polls audit log data from the Catalyst Center Intent API `/dna/intent/api/v1/audit/logs` every 5 minutes. Key fields: `auditRequestType`, `auditDescription`, `auditUserName`, `auditTimestamp`, `auditIpAddress`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:audit:logs" (auditRequestType="CONFIG*" OR auditRequestType="*PROVISION*" OR auditRequestType="*TEMPLATE*") | stats count as change_count values(auditDescription) as actions by auditUserName, auditIpAddress | sort -change_count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Configuration Change Audit Trail** — Configuration changes are the leading cause of network outages. A complete audit trail enables rapid rollback and root-cause identification.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses wildcard filters on `auditRequestType` to keep configuration, provisioning, and template activities.
• `stats` counts changes per `auditUserName` and `auditIpAddress` and lists distinct `auditDescription` values in the `actions` multivalue field.
• `sort -change_count` surfaces the heaviest changers for review or approval alignment.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions as required. Consider visualizations: Table (auditUserName, auditIpAddress, change_count, actions), timechart of config-related events, drilldown to raw events for ticket correlation.
grandmaExplanation: We record every configuration change made to your network through Catalyst Center, so you always know who changed what and when.
